title: Chrysanthemum grandiflorum
taxonomy: Chrysanthemum grandiflorum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, specifically within the genus Chrysanthemum. It is native to the mountainous regions of Asia. As a member of the Asteraceae family, it is closely related to other daisy-like plants. The species classification of Chrysanthemum grandiflorum is further divided into subspecies and cultivars. The classification of Chrysanthemum grandiflorum reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the plant kingdom.
uses: Chrysanthemum grandiflorum, also known as hardy chrysanthemum, has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its various health benefits. The plant's flowers, leaves, and roots are used to treat conditions such as fever, sore throats, and coughs. Its antiviral and antibacterial properties make it effective in combating infections and promoting wound healing. Additionally, Chrysanthemum grandiflorum is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. By incorporating Chrysanthemum grandiflorum into their treatment plans, individuals may experience improved overall health and well-being.

constituents: Chrysanthemum grandiflorum is a type of flowering plant traditionally used in various forms of medicine. Its active constituents include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. parts: Chrysanthemum grandiflorum, also known as hardy chrysanthemum or Chinese chrysanthemum, is a plant used in traditional medicine, particularly in China and Japan. The medicinal parts of this plant include its flowers, leaves, and roots, which are used to treat various health conditions. The flowers are rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenoids, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. intro: Chrysanthemum grandiflorum, also known as Yellow Chrysanthemum or Dried Chrysanthemum, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The health benefits of Chrysanthemum grandiflorum include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and act as an antioxidant, making it useful in the treatment of various cardiovascular and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Chrysanthemum grandiflorum is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into herbal capsules and tablets.
